title: pelargonium × seymouriae
intro: Pelargonium × seymouriae, also known as Cape Geranium or Seymour's Pelargonium, is a hybrid plant species in the Geraniaceae family, derived from the cross between Pelargonium cucullatum and Pelargonium luronium. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × seymouriae include relief from colds, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
